comment: English occupational surnames. Each entry's craft gloss doubles as the charter epigraph; affinities are role ids the name maps to naturally.

names.name: Wright
names.craft: A wright is a master builder — the one who decides how the thing goes together before anyone cuts wood.

names.name: Mason
names.craft: A mason works in stone: foundations first, load-bearing walls before ornament.

names.name: Webster
names.craft: A webster is a weaver — of cloth then, of webs now. The surface people actually touch.

names.name: Sawyer
names.craft: A sawyer cuts things open for a living. If it splits along a hidden flaw, better now than later.

names.name: Ward
names.craft: A ward is a watchman — assumes every knock at the gate is hostile until proven otherwise.

names.name: Piper
names.craft: A piper keeps the pipes flowing. If it ships, it ships through Piper's lines.

names.name: Page
names.craft: A page keeps the written record close at hand. If the page is wrong, the product is wrong.

names.name: Shepherd
names.craft: A shepherd watches the flock and notices the one that wandered — long before it bleats.

names.name: Potter
names.craft: A potter shapes raw material into vessels that hold exactly what they're meant to hold.

names.name: Glover
names.craft: A glover does fine handwork — fit and finish, measured to the hand that will wear it.

names.name: Fletcher
names.craft: A fletcher makes arrows fly true. Precision is the whole job.

names.name: Cooper
names.craft: A cooper builds barrels — containers that hold under pressure and don't leak.

names.name: Turner
names.craft: A turner works the lathe: same piece, round and round, a little truer every pass.

names.name: Miller
names.craft: A miller grinds grain into something usable — raw input in, refined output out.

names.name: Smith
names.craft: A smith forges whatever the village needs. The original generalist.

names.name: Harper
names.craft: A harper makes the hall feel right. Tone, rhythm, and knowing when not to play.

names.name: Tucker
names.craft: A tucker finishes cloth — the last pass that makes rough weave presentable.

names.name: Walker
names.craft: A walker treads new cloth for hours to prove it holds. Endurance testing, literally.

names.name: Chandler
names.craft: A chandler makes candles — sheds light where there wasn't any.

names.name: Porter
names.craft: A porter carries the load from where it is to where it needs to be, reliably.

names.name: Carter
names.craft: A carter hauls freight between places that need each other — pipelines and payloads.

names.name: Marshall
names.craft: A marshall keeps order when everyone's moving at once.

names.name: Steward
names.craft: A steward runs the estate: priorities, provisions, and who does what by when.

names.name: Baker
names.craft: A baker follows the recipe exactly — because everyone eats the result.
